comment_text.sn35.76:1.5: The following discourses have a brief question at this point, whereas the MS edition repeats the phrase “you should give up desire”. I follow the shorter version, found here in PTS and Bodhi.

root_text.sn35.76:1.2: ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā rādho bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.76:1.3: “sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā saṅkhittena dhammaṁ desetu, yamahaṁ bhagavato dhammaṁ sutvā eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto vihareyyan”ti.
root_text.sn35.76:1.4: “Yaṁ kho, rādha, aniccaṁ tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.76:1.5: Kiñca, rādha, aniccaṁ tatra te chando pahātabbo?
root_text.sn35.76:1.8: yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ. Tatra te chando pahātabbo …pe…
root_text.sn35.76:1.11: mano anicco. Tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.76:1.15: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccaṁ. Tatra te chando pahātabbo.
root_text.sn35.76:1.16: Yaṁ kho, rādha, aniccaṁ tatra te chando pahātabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn35.76:1.1: The Venerable Rādha went up to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn35.76:1.3: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.sn35.76:1.4: “Rādha, you should give up desire for what is impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.76:1.8: And the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is also impermanent. You should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.76:1.15: And the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is also impermanent. You should give up desire for it.
translation_text.sn35.76:1.16: You should give up desire for what is impermanent.”
